comment.text: This key displays a message within a dialog informing the user that paid AI services are being deactivated. It's used to explain the consequence of disabling AI services, likely in a settings or configuration dialog. The `{{aiServices}}` variable should be replaced with the specific name of the AI service.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Arabic translation uses the singular form 'المدفوع' (the paid [one]), which implies only one service is paid. The English source uses the plural 'paid {{aiServices}}'.

The Bulgarian translation uses a singular masculine form ('Платеният {{aiServices}} ще бъде деактивиран'), whereas the English source uses a plural form ('The paid {{aiServices}} will be deactivated'). Since {{aiServices}} represents multiple services, the Bulgarian translation should be plural.

Grammatical number mismatch: 'Platné {{aiServices}} bude deaktivováno' uses singular verb/adjective forms, but 'aiServices' (services) is plural. This changes the meaning from 'services will be deactivated' to 'service will be deactivated'.

The Greek translation uses the singular neuter form 'Το πληρωμένο {{aiServices}} θα απενεργοποιηθεί', which implies a single paid service. Since 'services' is plural in English, the Greek translation should use the plural form to maintain the correct meaning.

The Finnish translation uses the singular form 'Maksullinen' (Paid), but the English source uses the plural 'The paid {{aiServices}}'. Since {{aiServices}} is a variable that can represent multiple services, the adjective should ideally be pluralized or the sentence structure adjusted to avoid a grammatical mismatch in Finnish.
The English text uses the plural 'services', but the French translation uses the singular 'service'. This creates a grammatical mismatch when the variable {{aiServices}} contains multiple items.
The word 'Iegrofīninās' is not a valid Latvian word and appears to be a hallucination or a severe misspelling. The intended meaning 'will be deactivated' is not conveyed.

The Portuguese translation uses a singular noun phrase ('O {{aiServices}} pago será desativado'), whereas the English source uses a plural noun phrase ('The paid {{aiServices}} will be deactivated'). This creates a grammatical mismatch when the variable contains multiple services.

The Portuguese translation uses the singular form ('O {{aiServices}} pago será desativado'), whereas the English source is plural ('The paid {{aiServices}} will be deactivated'). This creates a grammatical mismatch when the variable contains multiple services.

The Romanian translation uses a singular form ('plătit va fi dezactivat'), whereas the English source uses a plural form ('paid {{aiServices}} will be deactivated'). This creates a grammatical mismatch if {{aiServices}} represents multiple services.

The Sinhala translation 'ගෙවන {{aiServices}}' literally means 'paying {{aiServices}}' (as in a person who pays) rather than 'paid {{aiServices}}' (services that have been paid for). This changes the subject of the sentence.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ගෙවීම් කර ඇති {{aiServices}} අක්‍රිය කරනු ඇත.

ai_spell_check_issues.description: Grammatical number mismatch: 'Platné {{aiServices}}' (plural) is paired with 'zostane deaktivované' (singular), which creates a logical error in Slovak. More importantly, the English 'The paid {{aiServices}}' implies the services themselves are paid, whereas the Slovak 'Platné' can be interpreted as 'The paid [amount/fee]...'. To match the English meaning of 'paid services', the noun must agree with the plural variable.

The word 'Zaplačna' is an incorrect adjective for 'paid' in this context. 'Zaplačna' refers to something related to paying/crying (from 'plačati' or 'plač'), whereas 'paid services' should be translated as 'plačane storitve'.
